reviews.review_description: Such a fun game, so frustrating when you’ve been going for ages then get hit!!! I wouldn’t mind if it was in mixed reality though as I keep getting to close to things in my living room. Well worth having.

Really enjoy.   Grandkids like it also,  I love that I can choose speed and amount of balls.   I like that its not just a typical hit the ball game, needing to match the colors added more to it.  
I wish there was more of a sound when I hit them together and a better way to shoot at the distance ones ( or I may not quite have figured that yet.)  Is there a way in settings to do this sitting if I wanted?

I find this game quite enjoyable. It engages both the hands and the brain simultaneously, and it's not that easy to finish a game. I would like the overall player ranking to be more visible. Additionally, I would have liked the developer to add real backgrounds of landscapes, whether it's the desert, mountains, or glaciers.

I love the game, but I always get the blue and purple confused since I am colorblind. I feel sad when I hit the wrong ball and lost scores due to colorblind.
I am looking forward to the further updates about colorblind friendly setting. Thanks for giving us such a good game.
I got the game and contacted the devs shortly after, response was pretty quick and they were super nice! Gameplay is just as good!

I love VR Fitness and use a lot of different games in my fitness regimen: punching, slicing, bow and arrow, jumping around. I am always curious about new offerings. I am glad I tried ColorBall VR. I am happy to report, it is unlike anything else!

It's more a game than fitness, though as the levels get harder I can feel it makes me move a lot. But there are a lot of things to pay attention to, so it will drain your mental energy as much as your physical.  If you find other boxing type workout games boring, you will find ColorBall a fun challenge.

The game is still under active development, but what's already included all works well. I am sure the user interface will get cleaner, at this point there is a lot going on with different machines, projectors, messaging boards (which you can actually draw on as well!!!). It will feel a lot, but the game does a good job at explaining everything.  

The game itself feels quite complete, and I haven't experienced any bugs or issues. Again there is a lot going on inside the game too, but all these guarantee you will not be bored while doing some exercise.

I recommend you to purchase it quickly, don't wait for the price to raise. Because I am sure as the game matures toward v1.00 the price deserves not to stay this friendly for long :) I have not regretted getting on this train early!

Updated: after more play, it just keeps getting better and better. The bow and arrow is so cathartic! Original: I have played this game quite a bit and it is really fun trying to top previous scores. I like the basic level best - weirdly relaxing, yet cathartic as you throw balls around the play space and pop balls. But the gravity wrist bands and light swords create a fun take on the game too - a completely different speed than many other sword based games. My favorite thing about the game is the speed - slow and steady. I look forward to other settings in the future.

developer_response.body:
THANKS for your review update! 😊
Happy to hear that you like the higher levels too 😃

reviews.review_description: Funny game. At first I was afraid it could be a little repetitive but after I started playing it and watched the tutorials I discovered lots of stuff that would make matches more appealing. I think the developer found a good way to extend the color match idea so that it becomes very joyful.

First, thank you developers for this challenging VR game, gameplay is good and fun but how do I know the ball is about to hit me ? , signal option to turn on or off , give me a signal or indication the ball is coming to hit me any part of my body if possible.  
Or options to define the hit area by heights x widths,  4 x 4 or 6 x 6 or 10 x 10
Can I play this in an endless mode ,  or no fall mode , or no hit mode . Or I just want to play to get more money to buy more equipments .  
 May be allow to choose beginner level with 10 hearts or normal level 5 hearts  or master level 3 hearts, just suggestions to improve the gameplay. 
I do recommend this game for everyone to enjoy. Thank you.

developer_response.body:
Many thanks for trying Colorball and your constructive feedback. You've played the very first test version of Colorball. So yes, we still have to improve the gameplay on some points and we have serveral plans for it. The hit area is a small area from your headset to the ground, but we will consider your suggestion of a variable hit area.

As our first reviewer, we will give you a free game key for your friends. Just mail us or follow us on instagram or twitter: www.colorballvr.com/#contact

reviews.review_description:
Colorball be is such a fun game to play 
It keeps you moving as you collect and match colours while dodging others 
This will keep you going always wanting to do better 

INSIDE  VR 
https://youtu.be/xpb6Y3QJr84

reviews.review_title: Great game that keeps you moving 🕺
reviews.review_description: I personally love the archery and boxing challenges... it's workout and fun 😃👍

Colorball is a fun game. It's not another beat game; the matching mechanic is quite unique in VR. There are a lot of elements to the gameplay - you can grab colorballs, smoosh them together, throw them, or holster them to save for later. There are also modifying modes with different tools to use to change up the gameplay. The vibe was surprisingly chill as opposed to frantic. But you can adjust the speed for more of a challenge. At the currently listed price of $4 I'd say Colorball is worth it, on the condition that some more levels are added as promised.

This is a really good game, I'm only eating this 4 stars for 2 reasons, 1st is because this isnt on the oculus store yet, and I would have loved to find this a lot sooner (no faults to the creators) and second is because it's hard to get started, and hard to get good (or I'm just bad at the game), but definitely worth it
I liked it and the graphics are beautiful :)

A simple concept involving matching colors is way more fun then I could have ever imagined. You really get your body moving a lot throwing, blocking, slashing. etc. 

Check out my video where I go into depth on my thoughts but this is definitely a great game to add to your collection.

Video : https://youtu.be/XoVkn5Qn3d0
I found this game very fun, much more than I expected to and it had loads of different varieties of levels and some visuals and backgrounds were impressive.

reviews.review_title: Balls with Boxing, Archery, Light sabers
reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed this workout of a game and look forward to unlocking the items, especially the bow. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mZHFsJOSLPQ

Typically, a game like this would not seem fun or challenging, but it is quite the opposite. This game was a lot of fun!

There are two playing environments -- a western desert with a train track or a Wild West rooftop. At the moment, I have only played a few missions that were accessible at the start. I had to collect and eliminate as many colored balls as possible within the time given. There are up to four spots on your body to hold four balls of any color (or weapons, depending on the round) to quickly grab the right color to use in the situation allowed. You can adjust both speeds of the balls and the number per round. The round ends if you get hit by any of them without catching or moving out of the way. 

There are also different playing modes. You have to eliminate identical colors, another where you use blue and green lightsabers or a bow and arrow, and lastly, one method where you have to punch balls of a specific color (depending on your hand color).

This game is very casual with a lot of depth to it, and it is a welcome change to something that you can pick up, play for a little bit, and then move on. I enjoyed the game so far and hope it only grows from here.

developer_response.body:
MANY THANKS for this great and detailed review 😊
We will keep new content coming 👍

reviews.review_title: Would you buy a coffee? Or this game?
reviews.review_description: This game is extremely fun. Out of all the games I own, this is one of the games I repeatedly come back to all the time because of how fun and addicting it is. For the price of this game. I very highly recommend it. This game is pretty much the same price of a cup of coffee. So here’s the question. Would you buy a cup of coffee, which is something you can only use once. Or Colorball VR? A game with addictive gameplay, hours of fun, and you get to keep forever.￼

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review! 😊

reviews.review_description: Very colorful game for all audiences in which you have to throw colored balls with their colored partner, and with more levels you have bows, lightsabers to break them also individually. An original way to exercise in an entertaining way and without dizziness. Ideal for beginners in VR and perfect for young children and adults. It is under development but it is a different proposal that is worth giving it a try. It has a very correct price.
